mechanism_narrative: A persistently high unemployment rate signals ongoing labor market weakness and reduced consumer spending capacity, which translates into lower corporate profits and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where UNRATE is above 7%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or near the zero lower bound, it indicates aggressive monetary easing by the central bank, which eventually stimulates economic activity and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS and SPX returns turns positive in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad-based collapse in consumer demand and investment, signaling a deep economic recession that will drive S&P 500 forward returns significantly lower.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show negative year-over-year growth
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 337677.0 and HOUST at 510.0 as of t-1mo/t-2mo
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 40% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST are contracting, or the interaction term fails to be statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing activity and overall supply-side distress, which directly impacts corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 returns in the short term.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth is negative for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the negative correlation between INDPRO decline and SPX returns disappears.

mechanism_narrative: A severe and persistent deterioration in both non-farm payrolls and the unemployment rate indicates a deep and structural labor market crisis, which will severely depress consumer confidence and spending, leading to prolonged negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132414.0 and UNRATE at 8.9% as of t-1mo
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 30% of periods meeting the trigger condition, or the joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE is not stronger than individual effects.

mechanism_narrative: Weak or negative real GDP growth signifies a contracting economy, which directly implies lower corporate revenues and earnings, thereby exerting downward pressure on equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where GDPC1 growth is negative, or the Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low inflation and extremely low federal funds rates suggests a disinflationary or deflationary environment where monetary policy has limited room to stimulate, leading to concerns about corporate pricing power and potentially negative S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 212.671 and FEDFUNDS at 0.15% as of t-1mo
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in > 50% of periods where both conditions are met, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A severe and sustained drop in housing starts below a critical threshold indicates a deep and prolonged downturn in the housing market, which has significant negative spillover effects on the broader economy and equity markets, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 600 thousand units (annualized) for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 40% of triggered instances, or the housing market recovers without a corresponding positive shift in SPX returns.
